items.headline: RBI launches key surveys for monetary policy; inflation and growth risks flagged
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) launched three key surveys (Inflation Expectations, Rural & Urban Consumer Confidence) to gather inputs for monetary policy. RBI Governor had previously indicated no immediate change to the inflation target. Retail inflation was 3.93% in May, below the 4% target, but is anticipated to surpass 4% in June due to higher food/fuel prices, geopolitical tensions, and concerns over weak monsoon/El Niño conditions, posing risks to inflation and growth.

items.headline: Indian markets set for higher open on improved global sentiment
items.summary: The Indian stock market is expected to open higher today, buoyed by improved global sentiment. This positivity stems from eased Middle East tensions following US-Iran peace talks, a strong overnight rally in US semiconductor stocks, and crude oil prices stabilizing around $71-72 per barrel. FPIs were net sellers (₹532.86 crore) while DIIs were net buyers (₹2,057.79 crore). Gift Nifty indicates a positive start above 24,000.

items.headline: TCS Q1 net profit rises 4.61% YoY, demand expected to improve
items.summary: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) reported a 4.61% year-on-year increase in consolidated net profit to ₹13,349 crore for the June quarter. Excluding exceptional items, net profit rose 8.5% YoY. Revenue climbed nearly 14% to ₹72,275 crore. TCS expressed optimism that demand, previously impacted by the West Asia conflict, is likely to improve in the ongoing quarter.

items.headline: Anand Rathi Wealth Q1 PAT up 24% YoY
items.summary: Wealth management firm Anand Rathi Wealth reported a 24% year-on-year rise in its consolidated profit after tax (PAT) to ₹116 crore for the June quarter of FY27.

items.headline: GM Breweries Q1 net profit surges 45.6%, revenue up 25.8%
items.summary: GM Breweries saw its net profit rise 45.6% year-on-year to ₹37.7 crore in Q1FY27, with revenue from operations increasing by 25.8% to ₹200 crore.

items.headline: Elecon Engineering, Indian Bank, L&T Finance, Just Dial, Bank of Maharashtra to announce Q1 results today
items.summary: Ten companies are slated to declare their June quarter numbers today, July 10, 2026. Notable among these are Elecon Engineering Company, Indian Bank, Bank of Maharashtra, L&T Finance, and Just Dial.

items.headline: Realty, Media, PSU Bank, Metal, Pharma sectors see buying interest; Auto, IT under pressure
items.summary: On July 9, 2026, sectors such as Realty, Media, PSU Bank, Metal, and Pharma witnessed buying interest, while IT and Auto remained under pressure. The automobile sector is anticipated to report significant revenue growth of 22-24% year-on-year in Q1 FY27, becoming a major contributor to overall corporate revenue growth. The Indian private credit industry is also projected for substantial expansion.

items.headline: Several companies including Axis Bank, Dr. Reddy's, Hindalco to trade ex-dividend; Goldiam International ex-bonus
items.summary: July 10, 2026, is an ex-date for dividends for multiple companies, including Axis Bank (₹1/share), Dr. Reddy's Laboratories (₹8/share), Hindalco Industries (₹5/share), Nestle India (₹5 final + ₹2 special), BSE (₹10/share), Apollo Tyres (₹2.50/share), Nucleus Software Exports (₹4/share), D-Link (India) (₹27.50/share), Nilkamal, JK Cement, Rossari Biotech, The New India Assurance Company, VST Industries, SOBHA, and Mahindra Logistics. Additionally, Goldiam International will trade ex-date for a 1:3 bonus issue.

items.headline: SAIL's Bokaro Plant to get 8.3 MT iron ore via slurry pipeline in ₹15,000 cr expansion
items.summary: Steel Authority of India Ltd (SAIL)'s Bokaro Steel Plant is set to receive 8.3 million tonnes of iron ore annually directly from SAIL mines through a modern slurry pipeline system. This initiative is part of an approximately ₹15,000 crore expansion project aimed at increasing crude steel production capacity.

items.headline: Dixon Technologies, Maruti Suzuki, JSW Steel, NALCO in focus today
items.summary: Dixon Technologies, Maruti Suzuki, JSW Steel, and NALCO are among the stocks expected to be in focus today due to market updates and upcoming earnings or related news.

streetRecommendations.name: Dalmia Bharat
streetRecommendations.rationale: projecting a 43% upside over the next 12-18 months
